sprint18AttestationCoverage.verificationMethod: Parsed apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/Sprint_plan.csv with python3 csv.DictReader filtering Target Sprint==18 AND Status==Completed -> 63 rows. Cross-referenced against `ls .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/*/attestation.json` presence (67 subdirs total; for-loop test -f check per subdir). 45 of the 63 completed IDs have attestation.json; 18 do not. This exactly matches the baseline claim of 45/63. Independently corroborated by docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md:11 (generated 2026-07-22T00:09:10Z), which lists the same 18 IDs among its system-wide 30-item 'completed without canonical attestation' list (the other 12 belong to non-Sprint-18 tasks: IFC-043, IFC-052, IFC-071, PG-110, PG-114, PG-127, PG-147, IFC-199, IFC-201, IFC-204, IFC-304, IFC-306).
sprint18AttestationCoverage.auxiliaryNote: 4 of the 67 attestation subdirs (DOC-015, DOC-016, IFC-211, IFC-257) do NOT correspond to a Target-Sprint-18+Status=Completed row: DOC-015 and DOC-016 are Target Sprint 18 but Status=Backlog (attestation verdict=COMPLETE exists ahead of the CSV flip); IFC-211 is Target Sprint 18, Status=Backlog, and its dir contains only task-tracking.json (no attestation.json); IFC-257 is Target Sprint 18, Status=In Progress, and IS attested (docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md explicitly calls this out as the one 'attested but not completed in CSV' case). 63 (completed-with-a-dir) + 4 (these) = 67 total subdirs.

meta.summary: Verified the baseline's core claim: exactly 45 of the 63 CSV-marked-Completed Sprint-18 tasks (Target Sprint=18) have a canonical .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/<ID>/attestation.json; 18 are missing (all INFRA-TF-* and the PG-196..209 tail). Root CLAUDE.md's headline sprint claim (line 3: 'Sprint 6 (MVP phase)') is stale by 12+ sprints against the actual current sprint (18-19) and the 316-task claim is stale against the current 597-row Sprint_plan.csv. The CLAUDE.md Sprint_plan split-file documentation (lines 34-43) is drifted on nearly every axis: Sprint_plan_J.csv exists on disk (8 rows, PG-210->AUTOMATION-003) and is completely undocumented, and 6 of the 9 documented A-I ranges/counts (B, C, D, E, G, I) no longer match the files on disk (I is the worst: documented as 26 rows, actually 46). docs/architecture/adr/README.md's index is similarly drifted: its 'Total ADRs: 48' metric (line 190) is stale against the 67 ADR files that exist (through ADR-068), and 15 real ADR files (ADR-049 through ADR-061, ADR-067, ADR-068) are completely absent from the index table, while ADR-055 is a genuine numbering gap with no consolidation note anywhere (unlike the properly-documented ADR-014->ADR-005 merge). The most severe single artifact found is .specify/sprints/sprint-18/_summary.json, generated 2026-04-11 and never regenerated since: it reports Sprint 18 as 3/43 tasks completed, which is off by more than 3 months and 60 tasks from the CSV-verified 63/67 reality reflected in docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md and docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md (both regenerated today, 2026-07-22). Attestation provenance is also uneven: 17 of the 45 present sprint-18 attestation.json files predate ADR-068 (2026-06-30, Attestation Provenance Standardization) and lack a `git_commit` field (4 of those also lack `attestation_timestamp` entirely), which ADR-068 itself acknowledges as a known, accepted historical gap rather than concealed fabrication.

findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:3 reads: '**IntelliFlow CRM** — AI-powered CRM system. Sprint 6 (MVP phase). IFC-010 Go/No-Go passed 2025-12-27. 316 tasks across 34 sprints tracked in `Sprint_plan.csv`.' Reproducible: `head -5 CLAUDE.md`. The actual current sprint is 18-19 per docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md:7 ('Active sprint: **Sprint 18** (sprint-18) — 94% complete') and the CSV now has 597 rows (`python3 -c "import csv;print(len(list(csv.DictReader(open('apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/Sprint_plan.csv')))))"` -> 597), not 316.
findings.impact: CLAUDE.md is the root Intent Node read at the start of every session per its own 'Session Continuation' rule; a stale sprint/task-count anchor at the very top of the file can mislead any agent or human skimming it before reading SESSION_CONTEXT.md, and undermines trust in the rest of the file's claims.
findings.rootCause: CLAUDE.md's opening line was written at Sprint 6 and never updated as the project progressed through 12+ subsequent sprints; unlike docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md (explicitly regenerated per session) CLAUDE.md has no regeneration discipline for this specific line.
findings.remediation: Update CLAUDE.md line 3 to reference the current sprint dynamically or drop the hardcoded sprint/task-count entirely in favor of pointing to docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md (which is already regenerated and already linked lower in the same file under 'Session Continuation').

findings.evidence: File reads: `"generated_at": "2026-04-11T16:48:01.958Z"`, `"task_summary": {"total": 43, "completed": 3, "backlog": 40, "blocked": 0, "attested_completed": 3}`. Reproducible: `cat .specify/sprints/sprint-18/_summary.json | head -12`. This contradicts the CSV-verified truth (63 of 67 Target-Sprint-18 rows are Status=Completed, computed via `python3 csv.DictReader` filter above) and contradicts docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md:7 ('Sprint 18 ... 94% complete (63/67 tasks)', regenerated 2026-07-22T00:09:10Z, i.e. same day as this audit).
findings.impact: Any tool or agent that reads _summary.json instead of Sprint_plan.csv/SESSION_CONTEXT.md for Sprint 18 status would report the sprint as 7% complete instead of 94% complete — a 87-point error that could trigger false blocker escalation or duplicate work.
findings.rootCause: _summary.json is a point-in-time snapshot generated once (2026-04-11) early in Sprint 18 and never regenerated as the sprint progressed to near-completion; no automated regeneration hook ties it to Sprint_plan.csv changes the way docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md's generator does.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-012 (CSV as Source of Truth) — a derived artifact 3.5 months stale relative to its source contradicts the spirit of the ADR even though the CSV itself was correctly updated.
findings.remediation: Either wire _summary.json regeneration into the same generator that produces docs/SESSION_CONTEXT.md/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md, or delete it and point consumers at the actively-maintained reports.

findings.evidence: 18 of 63 CSV-Completed Sprint-18 tasks have an attestation subdirectory but no attestation.json inside it: INFRA-TF-001..005, PG-196..199, PG-201..209. Reproducible: `for d in .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/*/; do id=$(basename "$d"); [ -f "${d}attestation.json" ] || echo "MISSING:$id"; done` then intersect with `python3 -c "import csv; rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/Sprint_plan.csv'))); print([r['Task ID'] for r in rows if r['Target Sprint']=='18' and r['Status']=='Completed'])"`. Independently corroborated by docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md line 11 (`CSV marks these tasks completed without a canonical attestation: ...`), which lists all 18 IDs.
findings.impact: 63 tasks are marked 'Completed' in the single source of truth (Sprint_plan.csv) but 18 of them (29%) have no independently-verifiable evidence file — the CSV's completion claim for those 18 tasks currently rests on the row's own Status column with no attestation cross-check.
findings.rootCause: The INFRA-TF-* and PG-196..209 batch appears to have been flipped to Completed in the CSV (bulk sync or manual edit) without the corresponding /exec-attestation Phase 5 step having been run per task, or the attestation.json files were never generated/committed for that batch.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-068 (Attestation Provenance Standardization) DoD requirement that PG-/IFC- tasks carry required provenance; INFRA-TF-* is not explicitly covered by ADR-068's PG-/IFC- scope, but the broader 'never mark Completed without evidence' project norm in CLAUDE.md's Critical Rules is at stake for all 18.
findings.remediation: Run /fix-incomplete-tasks or a dedicated reconcile-and-attest pass over the 18 listed IDs to either produce evidence-backed attestation.json files or flip the CSV Status back if the work cannot be verified.

findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:34-43 documents Sprint_plan splits A through I only (the tree diagram terminates at `└── Sprint_plan_I.csv # 26 rows (IFC-297 → IFC-308)`), but `ls apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/ | grep Sprint_plan` shows a 10th file, Sprint_plan_J.csv, exists on disk (8 rows: PG-210, IFC-314, AUTOMATION-003, ... verified via `wc -l` and `head -2`/`tail -2`). J is not mentioned anywhere in CLAUDE.md.
findings.impact: An agent following CLAUDE.md's explicit instruction 'DO NOT read Sprint_plan.csv directly ... Use split files' has no way to discover Sprint_plan_J.csv's existence from the documentation alone, and would silently miss the 8 tasks it contains (including AUTOMATION-003, a Sprint-18 task already referenced elsewhere in this audit).
findings.rootCause: `tools/scripts/split-sprint-plan.ts` (the documented regenerator) was run after CLAUDE.md's split-file table was last hand-edited, growing the CSV past 9 files' capacity without the corresponding CLAUDE.md table being refreshed.
findings.policyViolation: CLAUDE.md's own 'Sprint Plan' section instructs regenerating splits via the script but does not mention regenerating the table describing them.
findings.remediation: Regenerate the CLAUDE.md split-file table (rows/ranges for A-J) immediately after any `split-sprint-plan.ts` run, ideally via a small script rather than hand-editing, given how often it has already drifted (see STALE-005).

findings.evidence: Actual row counts/ranges (via `for f in A B C D E F G H I J; do file=.../Sprint_plan_$f.csv; echo "$f: $(($(wc -l <"$file")-1)) rows ($(sed -n 2p "$file"|cut -d, -f1) -> $(tail -1 "$file"|cut -d, -f1))"; done`): B=73 rows IFC-043->PG-016 (doc says 72 rows, IFC-043→PG-015, line 36); C=73 rows PG-017->PM-OPS-001 (doc says 73 rows PG-016→SALES-002, line 37); D=69 rows ENG-OPS-001->IFC-124 (doc says 69 rows PM-OPS-001→IFC-123, line 38); E=62 rows PG-126->IFC-170 (doc says 63 rows IFC-124→IFC-170, line 39); G=63 rows PG-157->IFC-232 (doc says 64 rows PG-157→IFC-233, line 41); I=46 rows IFC-296->PG-209 (doc says 26 rows IFC-297→IFC-308, line 43) — the worst drift, actual row count is nearly double the documented figure.
findings.impact: An agent trusting the documented A-I ranges to decide which split file contains a given Task ID (e.g. looking up IFC-124, IFC-233, or anything in the IFC-297..PG-209 span) will open the wrong file or miss the task, especially for split I where the boundary is off by 20 rows / 138 task IDs.
findings.rootCause: Same as STALE-004 — the split boundaries shift every time new tasks are inserted upstream in the CSV and `split-sprint-plan.ts` is re-run, but CLAUDE.md's row-count/range annotations were captured once and never refreshed in lockstep.
findings.remediation: Same remediation as STALE-004: regenerate the whole table (not just append J) from the live split files, or replace the static counts with a note to run `split-sprint-plan.ts --describe` (or equivalent) for current ranges.

findings.evidence: README.md:190-191 states: '**Total ADRs**: 48 (ADR-000 through ADR-048, minus ADR-014 consolidated into ADR-005)'. Reproducible: `ls docs/architecture/adr/*.md | wc -l` -> 70 files (67 numbered ADR-*.md + README.md + template.md + one schema-proposal doc), and `python3` glob-parse of ADR-\d+ in filenames shows numbers 0-68 present (min 0, max 68) minus the two gaps at 14 and 55, i.e. 67 real ADR files — 19 more than the '48' the metrics section claims, and the index now (as of ADR-068, dated 2026-06-30) extends far past ADR-048.
findings.impact: The 'Total ADRs' figure and the 'ADR-000 through ADR-048' range are both wrong; anyone using this section to gauge documentation coverage undercounts by ~40%.
findings.rootCause: The Metrics section was written when the ADR set stopped at ADR-048 and was never updated through the subsequent Sprint 15-18 ADR additions (049-068), even though the README's own body was patched piecemeal to add the 'Sprint 17 ADRs' and 'Sprint 15+ ADRs' tables further down.
findings.remediation: Recompute and replace the Metrics section's Total ADRs figure (67, or list the exact numbered range with its two gaps) as part of fixing STALE-007's missing-index-entries.

findings.evidence: 15 real ADR files exist on disk but appear nowhere in README.md's ADR Index tables: ADR-049-agent-plan-reflect-phases.md, ADR-050-duplicate-detection-runtime.md, ADR-051-public-product-tour.md, ADR-052-redis-monitoring-snapshot-bridge.md, ADR-053-n-plus-one-query-budget-detector.md, ADR-054-property-based-race-condition-testing.md, ADR-056-audit-hash-chain-concurrency.md, ADR-057-fast-pr-vs-full-regression-split.md, ADR-058-vitest-20-shard-strategy.md, ADR-059-ci-cache-strategy.md, ADR-060-required-checks-policy.md, ADR-061-test-impact-changed-test-policy.md, ADR-067-metrics-harness-dechurn-and-canonical-split.md, ADR-068-attestation-provenance-standardization.md. Reproducible: `for n in 049 050 051 052 053 054 056 057 058 059 060 061 067 068; do grep -c "ADR-$n" docs/architecture/adr/README.md; done` returns 0 for every one (all files themselves exist under `ls docs/architecture/adr/ADR-0{49..61}*.md docs/architecture/adr/ADR-06{7,8}*.md`).
findings.impact: The README, which is the documented entry point ('ADR Index') for discovering architectural decisions, is missing roughly a fifth of all real ADRs, including recent, high-relevance ones (ADR-064 Terraform SSOT is indexed at line 251, but ADR-067/ADR-068 which directly build on it are not). An agent or reviewer browsing README.md to find 'what decisions exist' would not discover these 15 ADRs.
findings.rootCause: README.md's ADR Index has been updated in batches tied to specific backfill efforts ('Retroactive ADRs', 'Sprint 14 ADRs', 'Sprint 15+ ADRs', 'Sprint 17 ADRs' — see README.md:200-253) but no batch was ever run for ADR-049 through ADR-061 or for ADR-067/068, even though those files exist and several (e.g. ADR-053 N+1 Query Budget Detector, ADR-054 Property-Based Race-Condition Testing) are referenced by name elsewhere in the repo per user memory notes.
findings.remediation: Add a 'Sprint 16-18 ADRs' (or similarly named) table entry for ADR-049 through ADR-061, ADR-067, and ADR-068, following the same backfill pattern used for the four existing batch tables.

findings.evidence: ADR-055 does not exist as a file (`ls docs/architecture/adr/ADR-055*` -> no match) and, unlike ADR-014 (explicitly documented at README.md:257 as 'merged into ADR-005 on 2026-04-15'), no 'Consolidated / Removed ADRs' entry or any other mention of ADR-055 exists anywhere in README.md (`grep -c ADR-055 docs/architecture/adr/README.md` -> 0) or elsewhere in docs/ (`grep -rn ADR-055 docs/` -> no matches).
findings.impact: Minor — a reader scanning ADR numbers sequentially (054, then 056) has no way to tell whether ADR-055 was withdrawn, renumbered, or simply never written, unlike the ADR-014 gap which is properly explained.
findings.rootCause: Likely an ADR number reserved/drafted and then abandoned before being committed, without following the same consolidation-note convention used for ADR-014.
findings.remediation: Add a one-line 'Consolidated / Removed ADRs' entry for ADR-055 (or confirm it was never allocated and note that), mirroring the ADR-014 entry.
findings.falsePositiveJustification: It is possible ADR-055 was intentionally skipped/never allocated rather than withdrawn after being written; without git history archaeology this cannot be fully distinguished from a true consolidation gap, though the total absence of any note is itself the finding either way.

findings.evidence: README.md:264 states 'Future ADRs use IDs **066+**.' but ADR-066 (metrics-tracking-cascade-and-derived-surface, dated 2026-06-14, already listed at README.md:253), ADR-067, and ADR-068 (dated 2026-06-30, README.md gap per STALE-007) already exist on disk — i.e. IDs 066, 067, and 068 are already allocated, so the note describing 066 as the *next* free ID is stale by at least 3 ADRs.
findings.impact: Low — a future ADR author consulting this line for the next free number would need to double-check against the filesystem anyway, but the note itself is misleading.
findings.rootCause: The note was accurate at the moment ADR-065 was the latest ADR and was not updated as ADR-066/067/068 were subsequently added.
findings.remediation: Update the note to 'Future ADRs use IDs 069+' (or remove the hardcoded number and instead say 'use the next unused ADR-NNN, see filesystem').

findings.evidence: 17 of the 45 present sprint-18 attestation.json files lack a top-level/`environment.git_commit` field, and 4 of those also lack `attestation_timestamp` entirely: IFC-212, IFC-227 (no timestamp either), IFC-234 (no timestamp either), IFC-248 (no timestamp either), IFC-309 (no timestamp either), IFC-310, IFC-311, PG-059, PG-060, PG-061, PG-062, PG-063, PG-180, PG-187, PG-188, PG-189, PG-190. Reproducible: `for f in .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/*/attestation.json; do echo "$f git_commit=$(grep -c '\"git_commit\"' "$f") timestamp=$(grep -c '\"attestation_timestamp\"' "$f")"; done`. Confirmed via direct read of .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/IFC-227/attestation.json, which has `completed_at` but no `attestation_timestamp` or `environment` block at all.
findings.impact: For these 17 tasks, the attestation.json cannot be tied to a specific commit SHA the way ADR-068's standard requires, weakening the evidentiary chain (though `artifact_hashes` with file-content SHA256s are still present in most, providing partial provenance).
findings.rootCause: ADR-068 (Attestation Provenance Standardization, ratified 2026-06-30) documents this exact issue as pre-existing debt: 'The fields were written ad-hoc — IFC-234 carries them, IFC-309 and PG-200 do not' (ADR-068 Context section), and explicitly chose NOT to retroactively invalidate the ~1000 historical attestations, making the optional-field schema deliberate rather than an oversight going forward. These 17 predate that standardization.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-068's six-field provenance block is 'required' by DoD only for PG-/IFC- tasks going through /exec-attestation post-2026-06-30; these 17 are historical and were explicitly grandfathered, so this is a documented gap rather than a policy violation, but is flagged here per the audit's attestation-provenance scope.
findings.remediation: No retroactive fix required per ADR-068's own decision (would not move the needle and risks further corpus churn); if desired, a best-effort backfill of `git_commit` from `git log --follow` blame on each attestation.json's own commit date could recover most of these.

findings.evidence: Line 5: '> ... **Last Updated**: 2026-04-16 (as of Sprint 17)' immediately followed by line 12: 'See `docs/design/content-audit.md` for the full counting rule. Verified: 2026-07-21.' Two different freshness claims (2026-04-16 vs 2026-07-21) coexist in the same document header block, and the '(as of Sprint 17)' qualifier is itself stale (current sprint is 18-19).
findings.impact: Minimal functional impact since the load-bearing figure (Total Pages: 211) is correctly current per the 2026-07-21 verification note and matches the docs-integrity-audit.ts canonical output, but the 'Last Updated' line is misleading on its own if read in isolation.
findings.rootCause: The DOC-015/DOC-016 reconciliation work (2026-07-21, per git log and .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/DOC-016/attestation.json) updated the canonical-count verification note but did not touch the older 'Last Updated (as of Sprint N)' header line above it.
findings.remediation: Update the 'Last Updated' line to 2026-07-21 (or later) and drop/update the '(as of Sprint 17)' qualifier to match, consolidating to a single freshness claim.

findings.evidence: DOC-015 and DOC-016 both have `attestation.json` with `"verdict": "COMPLETE"` (.specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/DOC-015/attestation.json:8 and .../DOC-016/attestation.json:8) but their Sprint_plan.csv rows still show Status=Backlog: `python3 -c "import csv; rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/Sprint_plan.csv'))); print([(r['Task ID'], r['Target Sprint'], r['Status']) for r in rows if r['Task ID'] in ('DOC-015','DOC-016')])"` -> both Target Sprint 18, Status Backlog.
findings.impact: The single source of truth (Sprint_plan.csv) undercounts real completion for these 2 tasks; any dashboard reading the CSV alone (rather than cross-referencing attestations) will show DOC-015/DOC-016 as not-yet-started work that is, per their own attestation evidence, already complete and merged.
findings.rootCause: Known, already-tracked lag: the DOC-016 attestation notes (line 148) explicitly say DOC-016 depends on DOC-015 landing on origin/main first, and the CSV flip for both was intentionally deferred pending merge sequencing — this matches the project's own record of the DOC-015/DOC-016 closure work as a pending CSV flip, not an unnoticed drift.
findings.policyViolation: CLAUDE.md 'Sprint_plan.csv is Single Source of Truth' rule — the CSV is, at the time of this audit, not yet reflecting the attested reality for these 2 rows.
findings.remediation: Flip DOC-015 and DOC-016 Status to Completed in Sprint_plan.csv (with Percent Complete=100) now that both attestations show verdict=COMPLETE, following the documented CSV-edit + sync process.

findings.evidence: Two sibling directories are both named 'runbooks' with different content: docs/runbooks/ (4 files: docker-ha.md, docs-integrity-gate.md, gate-4b-recovery.md, preship-guards.md) and docs/operations/runbooks/ (13 files: ci-cost-monitoring.md, dlq-triage.md, easypanel-runbook.md, ingestion.md, load-testing-local.md, maintenance-runbook.md, monitoring-runbook.md, notification-service.md, railway-deploy.md, release-checklist.md, terraform-hcp-backend.md, terraform-import.md, workers-runbook.md, workflow-troubleshooting.md). docs/runbooks/gate-4b-recovery.md itself cross-references docs/operations/runbooks/dlq-triage.md and docs/operations/runbooks/workflow-troubleshooting.md by full path, confirming both directories are live and intentionally distinct, not a duplicate/copy.
findings.impact: No functional breakage found (all cross-references use full disambiguating paths and resolve correctly), but the identical directory basename across two locations is a discoverability/naming hazard — 'the runbooks directory' is ambiguous when spoken or searched by directory name alone.
findings.rootCause: docs/runbooks/ appears to be a newer, narrower set (recent additions like docs-integrity-gate.md dated 2026-07-21) created alongside the pre-existing docs/operations/runbooks/ without renaming either.
findings.remediation: Consider consolidating into a single docs/operations/runbooks/ (or renaming docs/runbooks/ to something like docs/runbooks-gates/) to remove the ambiguity; not urgent since no broken references were found.

findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:3-4 claims '316 tasks across 34 sprints tracked in `Sprint_plan.csv`'. Reproducible: `python3 -c "import csv; print(len(list(csv.DictReader(open('apps/project-tracker/docs/metrics/_global/Sprint_plan.csv')))))"` -> 597 rows (nearly double), and `python3 -c "import csv; rows=list(csv.DictReader(open(...))); print(len(set(r['Target Sprint'] for r in rows))) "` shows sprint numbers extending well past 34 (Sprint_plan_J.csv alone contains PG-210/IFC-314/AUTOMATION-003 which are Target Sprint 18, but the CSV's Target Sprint values range from 0 into the high 20s/30s across 597 rows — docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md:6 independently states 'Numbered sprint scope: 30 sprints (0-29) plus 5 continuous tasks' and 'Overall progress: 457/597 tasks completed').
findings.impact: Same root cause and location as STALE-001 (both are part of the same stale opening sentence); the task-count claim is off by 281 tasks (89% understated), reinforcing that this line has not been touched since early in the project.
findings.rootCause: Same as STALE-001 — the opening sentence of CLAUDE.md was written once near Sprint 6 and never revisited.
findings.remediation: Fold into the STALE-001 fix — update or remove the hardcoded '316 tasks across 34 sprints' figure in the same edit.
